text: $TSEM highlights the growing gap between fundamentals and index-driven price action. The reported shipment of ~5M photonic ICs with $MRVL points to real AI infrastructure demand, while the sharp selloff tied to Russell 3000E Growth removal reflects mechanical flows rather than business deterioration. In these setups, fundamentals and technical index rules can diverge sharply in the short term, even if the long-term signal remains intact.

The most detailed map of the photonics stack you will find. 
 
Front-End: 
 
Photonic Integrated Circuits 
 
$POET 
 
$LWLG 
 
$MTSI 
 
Top Pick: $TSEM  
 
This Photonics Integrated Circuit (PIC) map targets the next phase of AI scaling: compute boundaries are now determined by optical interconnects, making PIC mass production the ultimate GPU enabler. 
 
Naming $TSEM (Tower Semi) as Top Pick is a masterclass. TSEM just officially announced a massive multi-year InP epiwafer supply pact with IQE today (June 15) to fortify its AI infrastructure roadmap. This complements its blockbuster $1.3B in contracted 2027 silicon photonics revenue and a record-breaking Q2, proving its operational dominance. 
While TSEM secures the foundational foundry alpha, early-stage innovators like $POET (unveiling its Blazar platform) and $LWLG offer massive asymmetric upside in external light sources.

$IQE 
My price view on IQE has actually been tracking pretty closely so far. 
 
And yeah, I’ve been bullish on this name for a while on X  not just when it moves up, but through the boring stretches too. 
 
What’s been interesting lately is the steady shift in how the business is being “locked in” by partners. 
 
$MTSI investment helps strengthen the balance sheet and supports long-term LTA demand for IQE’s InP epiwafers 
And now $TSEM has also come in with an LTA as of today 
 
It’s not just one signal anymore… it’s starting to look like repeat confirmations from different parts of the supply chain. 
 
Why I care about this? 
Because LTAs don’t usually show up when nobody cares  they show up when visibility on demand is improving. 
 
Still sub £1B market cap, but the way it’s being tied into the ecosystem feels more important than how the stock has been trading.

$XFAB 
Ok, let’s get real—people keep asking me about this one.  
 
$XFAB isn’t just a sleepy automaker supplier. They’re building a silicon photonics foundry to rival $TSEM and $GFS, and Europe is backing it. Even $NVDA is involved in evaluations behind the scenes. 
 
Here’s the play: 
R&D started 2023, finishing initial development around Oct 2026 
2027 = production ramp, 2028 = full-scale mass production 
Market thinks it’s just a depressed supplier, but this is really Europe’s push to build a local silicon photonics supply chain 
Risk/reward is front-loaded: if they commercialize, the upside could be massive 
 
For folks wanting less drama, $TSEM is the safer, de-risked route. But I’m keeping my eyes on $XFAB it’s early, but exciting.

$AEHR is still priced like a niche SiC burn-in play, but that framing may be off. 
 
HBM4E and high-bandwidth flash change the math. When you stack 12–16 dies, one bad die can kill a $1,000+ package. Post-packaging test becomes too expensive and too late. 
 
That pushes testing upstream. 
 
Wafer-level burn-in starts shifting from “nice-to-have QA” to economic necessity — and FOX-XP is already sitting right there. 
 
Zoom out: 
$MU drives HBM, 
$TSEM supports advanced interconnect layers, 
$AAOI moves data through optical systems. 
 
If AEHR ever ties into HBM/HBF qualification, this stops being an EV SiC story and starts looking like AI memory infrastructure leverage. 
 
Market isn’t pricing that yet.

This is probably one of the more important macro reads I’m tracking… 
 
Global photonics shifting from ~1.1T in 2025 → ~1.5T by 2030, and silicon photonics alone compounding ~4–5x this decade. Quantum photonics even faster in the background. 
 
But when you look at actual public exposure… it still feels early. Even if names like $AAOI $AEHR $TSEM $COHR $LITE double again from here, the combined market caps barely scratch what’s forming in optical interconnects, CPO, OCS, AI fabrics, and defense photonics. 
 
That gap between infrastructure scale and equity pricing is still wide. 
 
Not saying it closes fast… but it’s hard to ignore how under-owned this stack still is. 
 
Curious who else is building a basket here vs just watching it evolve?

Most people talking about “photonic memory” can’t explain CXL memory pooling, KV cache infrastructure, or how $PENG monetizes AI infrastructure. Seeing nonstop bad takes involving $RKLB, $HIMS, $TSEM, and $SMCI — mixing up MOCVD capex, system integration, and photonics IP like they’re the same thing. Too many AI buzzwords, not enough technical understanding. 
 
Pure gold! This post is a much-needed reality check, exposing those who conflate integrators like $SMCI with real photonics IP. 
 
AI inference is radically shifting from being compute-bound to memory-bound. If you don't grasp how $PENG's CXL-based 11TB KV Cache server breaks the "GPU memory wall" to eliminate redundant re-compute and slash TTFT, you can’t value true tech moats. Furthermore, dismissing $TSEM's massive $920M silicon photonics capex expansion without understanding MOCVD and epitaxial growth layers proves most are just chasing hype. 
 
 In the agentic AI era, what's your ultimate fix for the memory wall?
